wine_bars_late.name: Weinstube Kesselstatt
wine_bars_late.wine_program: The estate tavern of Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt, pouring the family's Mosel, Saar and Ruwer Rieslings in a 250-year-old palace beside the Liebfrauenkirche
wine_bars_late.tip: Sit in the garden under the cathedral and order a flight of Kesselstatt's Scharzhofberger Saar Rieslings beside the dry Mosel wines.
wine_bars_late.description: Historic estate Weinstube of Reichsgraf von Kesselstatt in the heart of Trier, pouring the family's Mosel, Saar and Ruwer Rieslings until midnight.

wine_bars_late.name: Weinsinnig
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A small, knowledgeable wine bar in the centre of Trier with a list built mostly on Mosel growers, poured by the glass with light plates
wine_bars_late.tip: The by-the-glass selection rotates through small Mosel estates; ask the staff to steer you to something off the main list.
wine_bars_late.description: Relaxed central-Trier wine bar focused on Mosel growers, open evenings Tuesday to Saturday with an uncomplicated, well-chosen by-the-glass list.

wine_bars_late.name: Die Mosel Vinothek & Winebar
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Around 150 characteristic Mosel wines in a listed 1890 wine-merchant's villa on the riverbank, with a wine bar pouring by the glass into the evening
wine_bars_late.tip: Friday to Sunday it opens from early afternoon; grab a riverside table and work through the Mosel-only by-the-glass selection.
wine_bars_late.description: Wine bar and vinothek in an 1890 merchant's villa on the Traben-Trarbach riverbank, pouring around 150 characteristic Mosel wines into the evening.

wine_bars_late.name: Tresor Wine & Bar
wine_bars_late.wine_program: The wine bar of the Ratskeller in the old town hall on Bernkastel's market square, pouring Mosel wines by the glass from early evening
wine_bars_late.tip: Open from 5pm Tuesday to Sunday right on the famous Bernkastel market square; ideal first stop before dinner in the old town.
wine_bars_late.description: Evening wine bar inside the Ratskeller on Bernkastel-Kues' market square, pouring Mosel Riesling by the glass from late afternoon.

wine_bars_late.name: Beim Weinbauer
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A wine and sparkling-wine bar in one of Cochem's oldest 16th-century houses, pairing Mosel wines and Sekt with small plates and tapas
wine_bars_late.tip: The 16th-century house is as much the draw as the list; order the tapas-style plates to graze through several Mosel pours.
wine_bars_late.description: Wine and Sekt bar in a 16th-century Cochem house, pairing Mosel wines with small plates and tapas in the evening.

wine_bars_late.name: Weinwirtschaft Friedrich-Wilhelm
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A wine bar and restaurant pouring a large selection from the Bischoefliche Weingueter, the Friedrich-Wilhelm-Gymnasium estate and the Rinke winery, open into the evening
wine_bars_late.tip: The list leans on Trier's historic church-linked estates; a good place to taste the Bischoefliche Weingueter range by the glass.
wine_bars_late.description: Trier wine bar and restaurant pouring a broad selection from the city's historic estates, including the Bischoefliche Weingueter, into the evening.

wine_bars_late.name: Burgblickhotel Weinbar
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A modern hotel wine bar pouring both established Mosel names like Dr. Loosen and emerging young growers, with a castle-view terrace
wine_bars_late.tip: Ask for the newcomer-grower flight; the bar deliberately balances classic estates against up-and-coming Mosel names.
wine_bars_late.description: Modern hotel wine bar in Bernkastel-Kues pouring established Mosel estates alongside emerging young growers, with a terrace facing Burg Landshut.

listening_bars.name: Wirtshaus Zur Glocke
listening_bars.wine_program: A snug, old-school Trier tavern serving regional wine and hearty food in a low-lit, conversational room open late every night
listening_bars.tip: This is a talking-and-drinking room, not a music venue; come for the unhurried, low-lit old-Trier atmosphere over local wine.
listening_bars.description: Cosy, low-lit old-Trier tavern pouring regional wine with hearty food until midnight, a quiet conversational room rather than a loud bar.

candle_lit.name: Weinstube Spitzhaeuschen
candle_lit.wine_program: The wine tavern of the Schmitz family vineyard inside a leaning 1416 cottage, pouring over 50 Mosel Rieslings and reds in a tiny candle-lit room
candle_lit.tip: The crooked 1416 pointed cottage is one of the most photographed buildings on the Mosel; squeeze into the upstairs nook with a glass of estate Riesling.
candle_lit.description: Tiny, candle-lit wine tavern in a leaning 1416 cottage off Bernkastel's market square, pouring the Schmitz family's Mosel Rieslings.

candle_lit.name: Alte Zunftscheune
candle_lit.wine_program: A warmly atmospheric evening restaurant and wine room in old Traben-Trarbach, pouring local Mosel wines from 5pm Tuesday to Saturday
candle_lit.tip: Book ahead; the candle-lit guild-barn room fills fast on weekend evenings in the heart of Traben-Trarbach.
candle_lit.description: Atmospheric evening wine room and restaurant in a historic Traben-Trarbach guild barn, pouring local Mosel wines from 5pm.

candle_lit.name: Doctor Weinstube
candle_lit.wine_program: A timber-framed hotel Weinstube in Bernkastel's old town named for the famous Bernkasteler Doctor vineyard, pouring Mosel Riesling in a beamed candle-lit room
candle_lit.tip: Order a glass of the namesake Bernkasteler Doctor, one of the Mosel's most storied single vineyards, in the half-timbered dining room.
candle_lit.description: Half-timbered hotel Weinstube in Bernkastel's old town, named for the Bernkasteler Doctor vineyard and pouring Mosel Riesling in a beamed candle-lit room.

candle_lit.name: Brueckenkeller
candle_lit.wine_program: A cosy cellar wine tavern by the Mosel bridge with views to Burg Landshut, pouring local Riesling in a low, candle-lit vaulted room
candle_lit.tip: Grab a riverside table at dusk for the floodlit Burg Landshut view, then move into the vaulted cellar for a nightcap Riesling.
candle_lit.description: Snug vaulted cellar wine tavern by the Mosel bridge in Bernkastel-Kues, pouring local Riesling with views toward Burg Landshut.

candle_lit.name: Weingut Stein - Haus Waldfrieden
candle_lit.wine_program: Ulli Stein's atmospheric tasting house above Alf, the venue for estate wine tastings and live-music and cultural evenings for small groups
candle_lit.tip: Evenings here pair Stein's dry slate Rieslings and rare Elbling with live music; tastings run for groups, so call ahead to join one.
candle_lit.description: Ulli Stein's candle-lit tasting house above Alf, hosting evening estate wine tastings with live music and cultural events for small groups.

lounges.name: Van Volxem Panorama Lounge
lounges.wine_program: The architect-designed vinotheque and panorama lounge of a leading Saar estate, with a lift up to a glass lounge overlooking the Saar loop and counter tastings of estate Riesling
lounges.tip: Ride the lift to the glass panorama lounge for the Saar-loop view, then taste the estate's mineral Wiltingen Rieslings at the counter.
lounges.description: Architect-designed vinotheque and glass panorama lounge of the Van Volxem estate above the Saar, with counter tastings of mineral Saar Riesling.

lounges.name: DEINHARD's Hotel Lounge
lounges.wine_program: The riverside lounge and bar of DEINHARD's hotel on the Bernkastel market house, serving Mosel wines and the hotel's own traditional-method Sekt
lounges.tip: Settle into the riverside lounge with a glass of the house Sekt before dinner; the hotel makes its own sparkling in the cellar below.
lounges.description: Riverside hotel lounge and bar at DEINHARD's in Bernkastel-Kues, serving Mosel wines and the hotel's own traditional-method Sekt.

lounges.name: Weingut von Othegraven Vinothek
lounges.wine_program: The estate vinothek of a 16th-century Saar house, now run by Guenther Jauch, pouring its Grosse Lage Riesling from the Kanzemer Altenberg and Wiltinger sites
lounges.tip: From May to October the vinothek opens Saturdays too; taste the Kanzemer Altenberg Riesling at one of the Saar's most beautiful estates.
lounges.description: Estate vinothek of the 16th-century von Othegraven house in Kanzem, now owned by Guenther Jauch, pouring Grosse Lage Saar Riesling.

late_tastings.name: Mosel-Vinothek (St. Nicholas Hospital)
late_tastings.wine_program: The largest vinotheque for Mosel Riesling in the world, in the vaulted cellars of the St Nicholas Hospital, where over 100 regional wines can be tasted
late_tastings.tip: Buy a tasting card and self-pour your way through more than 100 Mosel wines in the medieval hospital cellars; arrive mid-afternoon to avoid the tour crowds.
late_tastings.description: The world's largest Mosel-Riesling vinotheque, in the vaulted St Nicholas Hospital cellars in Bernkastel-Kues, with self-pour tastings of over 100 wines.

late_tastings.name: Weinhaus Hieronimi
late_tastings.wine_program: A wine cellar, wine cafe and Mosel garden in Cochem where the family's own Riesling and other Mosel wines are tasted with a view over the river
late_tastings.tip: The riverside garden is the spot for an early-evening estate tasting; the cellar pours the family's own Cochem wines.
late_tastings.description: Family wine cellar, cafe and Mosel garden in Cochem, with evening tastings of estate Riesling and other Mosel wines overlooking the river.

late_tastings.name: Lehnert-Veit Vinothek Moselgarten
late_tastings.wine_program: An idyllic estate vinothek directly on the Mosel and the cycle path in Piesport, pouring the family's Goldtroepfchen Rieslings for tasting
late_tastings.tip: Pull off the riverside bike path for a tasting of Piesporter Goldtroepfchen, one of the Mosel's most famous vineyard names, at its source.
late_tastings.description: Riverside estate vinothek in Piesport on the Mosel cycle path, pouring the family's Piesporter Goldtroepfchen Rieslings for tasting.

late_tastings.name: Bernkastel Strausswirtschaften (seasonal grower taverns)
late_tastings.wine_program: Seasonal pop-up grower taverns set up in winegrowers' courtyards and cellars in and around Bernkastel, pouring the family's own wine with simple home cooking
late_tastings.tip: A straw wreath or bouquet over a grower's door means the family is pouring their own wine that night; these Strausswirtschaften open in spring and autumn.
late_tastings.description: Seasonal grower-run pop-up taverns in courtyards and cellars around Bernkastel, pouring the family's own wine with home cooking in spring and autumn.

late_tastings.name: Bonsai & Wein - Vinothek der Saar
late_tastings.wine_program: A Saarburg vinothek pouring around 20 of the Saar's best growers, from Egon Mueller-Scharzhof and Geltz-Zilliken to Van Volxem and Peter Lauer, in self-pour tasting flights
late_tastings.tip: At a few euros for a three- or six-wine flight, it is the cheapest way to taste cult Saar names like Egon Mueller side by side.
late_tastings.description: Saarburg vinothek pouring around 20 top Saar growers including Egon Mueller and Geltz-Zilliken, with inexpensive self-pour tasting flights.

late_tastings.name: Weingut Dr. Wagner Vinothek
late_tastings.wine_program: Tastings of estate Saar Riesling and Sekt in an 1898 turreted manor, made from steep slate sites by a fifth-generation Saarburg family
late_tastings.tip: Taste in the grand 1898 manor with its turret and sweeping staircase; the estate's Saarburger Rausch Rieslings are the highlight.
late_tastings.description: Saar estate vinothek in an 1898 turreted Saarburg manor, with tastings of steep-slate Riesling and Sekt from a fifth-generation family.

late_tastings.name: Vinothek Wiltingen
late_tastings.wine_program: A village vinothek in Wiltingen showcasing the fine, delicate Rieslings of the Saar's steep slate sites for tasting and purchase
late_tastings.tip: Pair a stop here with the Van Volxem lounge next door on the Schlossberg for a full Wiltingen-Saar afternoon.
late_tastings.description: Village vinothek in Wiltingen showcasing the fine, delicate Rieslings of the Saar's steep slate sites for tasting and purchase.

sparkling_rooms.name: DEINHARD's Sektwerkstatt
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: A hotel sparkling-wine manufactory in the historic cellar at Bernkastel's market house, making and pouring traditional bottle-fermented Riesling Sekt
sparkling_rooms.tip: Book a Sekt tasting in the cellar to taste traditional-method Riesling sparkling made on site, a German answer to grower Champagne.
sparkling_rooms.description: Hotel sparkling-wine manufactory in DEINHARD's historic Bernkastel cellar, making and pouring traditional bottle-fermented Riesling Sekt.

sparkling_rooms.name: Weingut Bindges Vinothek Rosenhof
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: A modern estate vinothek in the historic Rosenhof, pouring Bindges Mosel Riesling alongside traditional bottle-fermented Riesling Sekt and Secco
sparkling_rooms.tip: Pair the still Rieslings with the estate's traditional-method Sekt; the Secco is the easy-drinking summer-terrace option.
sparkling_rooms.description: Modern estate vinothek in the Traben-Trarbach Rosenhof, pouring Bindges Mosel Riesling with traditional-method Riesling Sekt and Secco.

sparkling_rooms.name: Sekthaus Lieser
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: A dedicated Mosel Sekt house refining selected Mosel wines into traditional-method sparkling, with tastings of Riesling and Pinot Sekt
sparkling_rooms.tip: A rare Mosel address focused purely on sparkling; taste the Riesling Sekt to see how the region's racy acidity suits traditional-method fizz.
sparkling_rooms.description: Dedicated Mosel Sekt house in Lieser refining selected wines into traditional-method sparkling, with tastings of Riesling and Pinot Sekt.
